title: ficus nervosa subsp. nervosa
intro: Ficus nervosa subsp. nervosa, also known as Indian fig or sacred fig, is a subspecies of plant in the fig genus Ficus, native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of Ficus nervosa subsp. nervosa include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments. The health benefits of this plant include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep, and alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome and digestive issues. Ficus nervosa subsp. nervosa is traditionally prepared as a tea, powder, or tincture, and is also used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to promote overall well-being and balance.
